info.description: Unified social media inbox API. Read and respond to comments, DMs, reviews, and mentions across Instagram, Facebook, Threads, Google Business Profile, TikTok, and LinkedIn through a single REST API. YouTube, X/Twitter, and Trustpilot coming soon.
info.title: SocialAPI.AI

get.description: Returns all active social media accounts connected to the authenticated API key's workspace.

post.description:
Initiates account connection. For OAuth2 platforms returns an auth_url to redirect the user to.
For apikey/credentials platforms stores the credentials immediately and returns account_id.

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a connected account. It will no longer appear in the accounts list
and all API calls using this account will return 404.

get.description: Fetches direct message threads for a connected account, sorted by most recently active. Each thread is returned as an `Interaction` with `type: "dm"`. Consumes credits per your plan.

parameters.description: Return only threads active after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns the thread ID for an existing DM conversation with the given platform user. Returns 404 if no prior conversation exists with that user. Use the returned `thread_id` to send messages via `POST /accounts/:id/dms/:tid/send`. Consumes credits per your plan.
get.summary: Look up a DM thread by user ID

parameters.description: Platform-specific user ID to look up
parameters.name: user_id

200.description: Thread ID for the existing conversation

403.description: DMs not available on your plan

404.description: Account not found or no prior conversation with this user

501.description: Platform does not support DM thread lookup

post.description: Sends a message to an existing DM thread. Use `GET /accounts/:id/dms/thread?user_id=...` to look up the thread ID before calling this endpoint. Consumes credits per your plan.

X-Interactions-Remaining.description: Interactions remaining this period (Free plan only)

429.description: Monthly interaction limit exceeded or platform rate limit hit

501.description: Platform does not support sending DMs

post.description: Hides, unhides, or permanently deletes a comment. The `:iid` must be a comment ID (prefixed `sapi_cmt_`). Deleted comments cannot be recovered.

501.description: Platform does not support comment moderation

get.description: Fetches the reply thread for a specific comment. The `:iid` must be a comment ID (prefixed `sapi_cmt_`).

parameters.description: Return only items created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

400.description: Invalid interaction ID or query parameters

501.description: Platform does not support reply threads

post.description: Posts a reply to a comment (`sapi_cmt_`) or review (`sapi_rev_`). The interaction type is automatically detected from the ID prefix. Set `private: true` to send the reply as a private/direct message on platforms that support it (e.g. Instagram).
post.summary: Reply to a comment or review

X-Interactions-Remaining.description: Interactions remaining this period (Free plan only)

400.description: Invalid body or unsupported interaction type

429.description: Monthly interaction limit exceeded or platform rate limit hit

get.description: Returns platform-specific quota data for a connected account as a key/value map where values are remaining counts. Keys are platform-defined — for example Instagram returns `posts_remaining`. An empty object is returned for platforms without usage limits.

501.description: Platform does not expose usage limits

get.description: Fetches posts and stories where your brand account was mentioned or tagged. Results are sorted newest-first. Returns 501 for platforms that do not support mention tracking.

parameters.description: Return only items created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

501.description: Platform does not support mention tracking

get.description: Fetches comments for a specific post on a connected account. Results are sorted newest-first. Use `cursor` for pagination and `since` to poll for new comments incrementally.

parameters.description: Return only items created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

429.description: Daily rate limit exceeded or platform rate limit hit

501.description: Platform does not support this operation

post.description: Toggles the comments setting for a specific post. Set `enabled: false` to prevent new comments; `enabled: true` to re-open them. Returns 501 on platforms that do not expose this setting.
post.summary: Enable or disable comments on a post

501.description: Platform does not support toggling comments

get.description: Fetches reviews for a connected account (e.g. Google Business, Trustpilot). Results are sorted newest-first. Returns 501 for platforms that do not support reviews (e.g. Instagram).

parameters.description: Return only items created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns all API keys for the authenticated user.

post.description: Generates a new API key for the authenticated user.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing name

delete.description: Deactivates an API key. This is irreversible.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of uploaded media files for the authenticated user.
Returns total bytes used, the plan limit, and file count for the authenticated user.

post.description: Upload a media file directly through the API server.

get.description:
Returns a presigned S3 PUT URL for client-side media upload. Upload the file directly
via HTTP PUT to the returned URL, then call POST /v1/media/:id/verify to confirm.
get.summary: Get a presigned media upload URL

post.description: Confirms that a presigned-URL upload completed successfully and marks the media as ready.

Exchanges an authorization code (received from the platform callback) for tokens.
Validates the state token against Redis to prevent CSRF. State is one-time-use.

403.description: State belongs to a different API key

get.description: Returns posts with rich filtering and cursor pagination. Supports filtering by status, platform, account_ids, date range, and text search.

parameters.description: Filter by status (draft, scheduled, publishing, published, partial, failed, cancelled)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Sort order: scheduled_asc (default), scheduled_desc, created_asc, created_desc
parameters.name: sort

post.description:
Publishes to one or more connected accounts. If scheduled_at is omitted the post is
published immediately (synchronous — blocks until all accounts respond). If scheduled_at
is set the post is queued for future publication (asynchronous). Omit account_ids and
scheduled_at to create a draft. Consumes 1 post credit.

201.description: All accounts published (sync) or post scheduled/drafted

207.description: Some accounts published, some failed (sync only)

post.description: Upload a CSV file to create multiple posts. Use dry_run=true to validate without creating.

get.description: Returns publishing attempt logs with optional filtering by status, platform, and action.
Returns media and text constraints for all supported platforms. Cacheable.

post.description: Dry-run validation. Returns errors for hard limit violations and warnings for best-practice issues.

get.description: Fetch a post by its ID with all platform delivery details.

delete.description:
Without ?platform: cancels a scheduled post or deletes a failed/published post across all platforms.
With ?platform=<name>: unpublishes from that single platform only (must be in published state).
The post record is kept; its status is recalculated based on remaining platform deliveries.
delete.summary: Delete, cancel, or unpublish a post

parameters.description: Unpublish from this platform only (e.g. instagram, facebook)
parameters.name: platform

400.description: Platform not found on post or not in published state

patch.description:
Updates the text, media, schedule time, target accounts, or hidden status of a pending post.
Only posts in `draft`, `scheduled`, or `failed` status can be updated.

get.description: Returns all publishing attempt logs for a specific post.
Fetches live engagement metrics from platform APIs for a published post.

post.description: Re-attempts publication of a post in `failed` or `partial` status. Consumes 1 post credit.

get.description: Full-text search across all platforms and interaction types. Accepts query param `q` for the search term; optionally filter by `platform` and `type`. Fan-out to all relevant connectors with merged, ranked results. **Not yet implemented** — returns 501.

Returns accounts, posts, and interactions consumed during the current billing period alongside the plan limits. -1 means unlimited (all paid plans). Period boundaries come from Stripe subscription or rolling 30-day windows for free users.
get.summary: Get resource usage for the current billing period

200.description: Resource usage for the current billing period

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile (id, email, plan tier, onboarding flag).

delete.description: Permanently deletes the authenticated user account and all associated data (API keys, connected accounts, usage logs). This action is irreversible.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's profile. Currently only supports setting onboarding=false to mark onboarding as complete.

400.description: Invalid request body or onboarding value

get.description: Returns all webhook endpoints for the authenticated user.

post.description: Registers a new webhook endpoint. The generated secret is returned once — store it securely.

delete.description: Removes a webhook endpoint. Future events will not be delivered to this URL.

patch.description: Partially updates a webhook endpoint. All fields are optional; at least one must be provided.

BearerAuth.description: Prefix your API key with "Bearer ". Example: `Authorization: Bearer sapi_key_...`
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

metadata.description: Metadata contains platform-specific connection data (e.g. redirect_uri for OAuth2 platforms).

platform.description: Platform identifies the social platform to connect (e.g. instagram, facebook, google, threads, tiktok, linkedin).

message.example: Store this key securely. It will not be shown again.

account_ids.description: AccountIDs lists the connected accounts to publish to. At least one is required.

media_ids.description: MediaIDs references previously uploaded media files to attach.

platform_options.description: PlatformOptions passes platform-specific settings (e.g. topicType for Google).

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt schedules the post for future publication. Omit for immediate publish.

text.description: Text is the post caption / body. Required.

code.description: Code is the authorization code received from the platform callback.

metadata.description: Metadata must include redirect_uri and state for CSRF validation.

platform.description: Platform identifies the social platform (must match the connect call).

action.description: Action must be one of: hide, unhide, delete.

private.description:
Private sends the reply as a private/direct message when supported by the platform.
Ignored for review replies.

text.description: Text is the reply content. Required.
text.example: Thanks for your feedback! We're glad you enjoyed it.

text.description: Text is the message content. Required.
text.example: Hi! How can we help you today?

enabled.description: Enabled enables comments when true, disables them when false.

hidden.description: Hidden hides or unhides the post from list responses.

media_ids.description: MediaIDs replaces the media files attached to the post.

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt changes the scheduled publication time.

text.description: Text is the updated post caption / body.

text.example: Love this product! Really glad I found it.
